body.u: At nineteen I left school after my exams were over in June.
In September of 1978 I started my first job as a mechanical fitter at a nearby garage.

I was employed to fix cars and in all aspects of the job I succeeded. At the age of twenty I finished my four year apprenticeship and continued at the garage for another four years until I saved up enough money to buy a small holding about ten miles from my first house.
I left the garage that year and established my own garage after about six months hard work and business started coming in quick.

By the age of thirty I had built my own extension onto the garage and was now employing three full time mechanics which included one driver for the rescue service and one for the motorcycle shop on the premises.

That year I got married and my wife was kept busy as a personal secretary, as well as a housekeeper. We had a four bedroomed detached house at the back of the garage and two acres of land as well. A year later my wife gave birth to twins, boys, and she then had her hands full with housework.
By that time I had a few thousand stashed away even though we were living well.

At the age of forty even my two sons left school, one of them became an apprentice electrician at the dockyard and the other worked as a bank clerk. Five years later I had earnt enough money to sell up and retire. I bought myself a camper van and a little two bedroom bungalow just down the road. I sold up the garage, complete with the house and put it all into the bank, the money I earnt.
After selling up the business my wife and I went on a world cruise then settled down to a quiet retirement. My two sons got married at twenty and twenty one and went to live abroad with their aunts.
My wife and I went touring. In the summer and the rest of the time was spent in the garden, house or at friend’s houses.
At sixty five my wife died of cancer and now I live on my own, with my memories and my house and home.
